event_summary: Pfizer vaccine efficacy news and U.S. election transition period supported global risk-on; classify as broad risk-on, not panic.
Omicron variant uncertainty drove global COVID risk repricing around early Dec 2021; low/mid-low volume down week needs panic-vs-accumulation review.
Global equities were under pressure from inflation/Fed tightening and growth-stock selloff; low-price high-volume down week may include panic selling.
Russia-Ukraine war shock and commodity/oil risk dominated markets; low-price high-volume down week more likely panic/liquidation first, later rebound possible.
Continuation of Russia-Ukraine shock; data shows low-price extreme-volume down week followed by positive 4w return, possible panic capitulation rather than ordinary accumulation.
2022 inflation/Fed tightening and global recession concerns; low-price extreme-volume up week may be rebound after risk-off selling.
Late Sep 2022 had strong dollar/Fed hike pressure and Japan yen intervention context; low-price extreme-volume down week may be macro panic/FX stress.
SVB collapse and Credit Suisse stress dominated global bank risk; extreme-volume down week likely event-risk/liquidity stress.
BOJ July 28 2023 YCC flexibility shift lifted JGB yields and affected Japanese equities; high-price extreme-volume down week may be policy repricing/distribution risk.
BOJ hike plus yen carry trade unwind triggered historic Japan/global selloff and rebound; low/mid-low extreme-volume up week likely panic reversal/capitulation rebound.
Early Apr 2025 U.S. tariff shock hit Japan/global equities; low-price extreme-volume down week likely tariff-driven panic/risk-off.
Tariff-driven volatility continued; low-price extreme-volume up week followed by strong 4w return, possible panic capitulation plus policy-expectation rebound.
